{{Infobox ancient site | name = Yokotashimo Kofun | native_name =横田下古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yokotashimo Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33|26|04|N|130|02|01|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Karatsu, Saga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|04|N|130|02|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yokotashimo Kofun'''|横田下古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the [[Hamatama, Saga|Hamatama]] neighborhood of the city of [[Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[Saga Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/192012|title=横田下古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yokotashimo Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular burial mound, with an estimated diameter of about 30 meters, located on a hill about 60 meters above sea level at the eastern foot of Mount Kagamiyama, which is located almost in the center of the Karatsu Plain facing Karatsu Bay. It was discovered in 1924 when the owner of the land dug up the passage to the stone chamber while clearing a forest, and discovered the grave goods when he opened the sarcophagus inside. The horizontal-type stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the south and is divided into a passageway and a rear chamber. The rear chamber is about 4 meters long, 2.25 meters wide and 2.34 meters high and gradually narrows towards the top, reaching a ceiling on consisting of three huge stones. It was constructed using broken basalt stones and granite, and the entire wall is painted with red pigment. Part of the western wall of the passageway has been destroyed, but what is thought to be the gate remains in its original state and is blocked by a single slab of stone. Inside the burial chamber are three stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]] made of flat slabs, with the first stone sarcophagus adjacent to the back wall having a cover stone, and the stone on the south side forming the northern wall of the second stone sarcophagus which is parallel to it, and the third stone sarcophagus placed separately, parallel to the western wall of the antechamber. [[Grave goods]] include a round and a square [[bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'' in the first sarcophagus, cylindrical copperware, iron arrowheads, and a short armor in the second sarcophagus, and [[Haji ware]] pottery in the third sarcophagus, as well as the bones of eight people. The burial mound is of a common form, but the style of the burial chamber and layout of the stone coffins is unique in ''kofun'' which have been excavated to date. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The grave goods designated are a Saga Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031524/|title=佐賀文化財 database|language=ja |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from [[Hamasaki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031667/ Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | name = Yokotashimo Kofun | native_name =横田下古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yokotashimo Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33|26|04|N|130|02|01|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Karatsu, Saga]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|04|N|130|02|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yokotashimo Kofun'''|横田下古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Hamatama, Saga|Hamatama]] neighborhood of the city of [[Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[Saga Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/192012|title=横田下古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yokotashimo Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun, with an estimated diameter of about 30 meters, located on a hill about 60 meters above sea level at the eastern foot of Mount Kagamiyama, which is located almost in the center of the Karatsu Plain facing Karatsu Bay. It was discovered in 1924 when the owner of the land dug up the passage to the stone chamber while clearing a forest, and discovered the grave goods when he opened the sarcophagus inside. The horizontal-type stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the south and is divided into a passageway and a rear chamber. The rear chamber is about 4 meters long, 2.25 meters wide and 2.34 meters high and gradually narrows towards the top, reaching a ceiling on consisting of three huge stones. It was constructed using broken basalt stones and granite, and the entire wall is painted with red pigment. Part of the western wall of the passageway has been destroyed, but what is thought to be the gate remains in its original state and is blocked by a single slab of stone. Inside the burial chamber are three stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]] made of flat slabs, with the first stone sarcophagus adjacent to the back wall having a cover stone, and the stone on the south side forming the northern wall of the second stone sarcophagus which is parallel to it, and the third stone sarcophagus placed separately, parallel to the western wall of the antechamber. [[Grave goods]] include a round and a square [[bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'' in the first sarcophagus, cylindrical copperware, iron arrowheads, and a short armor in the second sarcophagus, and [[Haji pottery]] pottery in the third sarcophagus, as well as the bones of eight people. The Kofun is of a common form, but the style of the burial chamber and layout of the stone coffins is unique in ''kofun'' which have been excavated to date. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The grave goods designated are a Saga Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031524/|title=佐賀文化財 database|language=ja |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from [[Hamasaki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031667/ Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from [[Hamasaki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Yokotashimo Kofun'''|横田下古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[Hamatama, Saga|Hamatama]] neighborhood of the city of [[Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[Saga Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/192012|title=横田下古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yokotashimo Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun, with an estimated diameter of about 30 meters, located on a hill about 60 meters above sea level at the eastern foot of Mount Kagamiyama, which is located almost in the center of the Karatsu Plain facing Karatsu Bay. It was discovered in 1924 when the owner of the land dug up the passage to the stone chamber while clearing a forest, and discovered the grave goods when he opened the sarcophagus inside. The horizontal-type stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the south and is divided into a passageway and a rear chamber. The rear chamber is about 4 meters long, 2.25 meters wide and 2.34 meters high and gradually narrows towards the top, reaching a ceiling on consisting of three huge stones. It was constructed using broken basalt stones and granite, and the entire wall is painted with red pigment. Part of the western wall of the passageway has been destroyed, but what is thought to be the gate remains in its original state and is blocked by a single slab of stone. Inside the burial chamber are three stone [[sarcophagus|sarcophagi]] made of flat slabs, with the first stone sarcophagus adjacent to the back wall having a cover stone, and the stone on the south side forming the northern wall of the second stone sarcophagus which is parallel to it, and the third stone sarcophagus placed separately, parallel to the western wall of the antechamber. [[Grave goods]] include a round and a square [[bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'' in the first sarcophagus, cylindrical copperware, iron arrowheads, and a short armor in the second sarcophagus, and [[Haji pottery]] pottery in the third sarcophagus, as well as the bones of eight people. The Kofun is of a common form, but the style of the burial chamber and layout of the stone coffins is unique in ''kofun'' which have been excavated to date. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The grave goods designated are a Saga Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031524/|title=佐賀文化財 database|language=ja |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from [[Hamasaki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031667/ Saga Prefecture home The horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south and is divided into a passageway and a rear chamber. The rear chamber is about 4 meters long, 2.25 meters wide and 2.34 meters high and gradually narrows towards the top, reaching a ceiling on consisting of three huge stones. It was constructed using broken basalt stones and granite, and the entire wall is painted with red pigment. Part of the western wall of the passageway has been destroyed, but what is thought to be the gate remains in its original state and is blocked by a single slab of stone. Inside the burial chamber are three stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]] made of flat slabs, with the first stone sarcophagus adjacent to the back wall having a cover stone, and the stone on the south side forming the northern wall of the second stone sarcophagus which is parallel to it, and the third stone sarcophagus placed separately, parallel to the western wall of the antechamber. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a round and a square [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'' in the first sarcophagus, cylindrical copperware, iron arrowheads, and a short armor in the second sarcophagus, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery in the third sarcophagus, as well as the bones of eight people. The Kofun is of a common form, but the style of the burial chamber and layout of the Sarcophaguss is unique in ''kofun'' which have been excavated to date. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The grave goods designated are a Saga Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031524/|title=佐賀文化財 database|language=ja |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Hamasaki Station|Hamasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yokotashimo Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yokotashimo Kofun | native_name =横田下古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yokotashimo Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33|26|04|N|130|02|01|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu, Saga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|04|N|130|02|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yokotashimo Kofun'''|横田下古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Hamatama, Saga|Hamatama]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/192012|title=横田下古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yokotashimo Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun, with an estimated diameter of about 30 meters, located on a hill about 60 meters above sea level at the eastern foot of Mount Kagamiyama, which is located almost in the center of the Karatsu Plain facing Karatsu Bay. It was discovered in 1924 when the owner of the land dug up the passage to the stone chamber while clearing a forest, and discovered the grave goods when he opened the sarcophagus inside. The horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south and is divided into a passageway and a rear chamber. The rear chamber is about 4 meters long, 2.25 meters wide and 2.34 meters high and gradually narrows towards the top, reaching a ceiling on consisting of three huge stones. It was constructed using broken basalt stones and granite, and the entire wall is painted with red pigment. Part of the western wall of the passageway has been destroyed, but what is thought to be the gate remains in its original state and is blocked by a single slab of stone. Inside the burial chamber are three stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]] made of flat slabs, with the first stone sarcophagus adjacent to the back wall having a cover stone, and the stone on the south side forming the northern wall of the second stone sarcophagus which is parallel to it, and the third stone sarcophagus placed separately, parallel to the western wall of the antechamber. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a round and a square [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'' in the first sarcophagus, cylindrical copperware, iron arrowheads, and a short armor in the second sarcophagus, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery in the third sarcophagus, as well as the bones of eight people. The Kofun is of a common form, but the style of the burial chamber and layout of the Sarcophaguss is unique in ''kofun'' which have been excavated to date. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The grave goods designated are a Saga Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031524/|title=佐賀文化財 database|language=ja |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Hamasaki Station|Hamasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031667/ Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The Kofun is of a common form, but the style of the burial chamber and layout of the Sarcophaguss is unique in ''kofun'' which have been excavated to date. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The grave goods designated are a Saga Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031524/|title=佐賀文化財 database|language=ja |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Hamasaki Station|Hamasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|'''Yokotashimo Kofun'''|横田下古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Hamatama, Saga|Hamatama]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/192012|title=横田下古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yokotashimo Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun, with an estimated diameter of about 30 meters, located on a hill about 60 meters above sea level at the eastern foot of Mount Kagamiyama, which is located almost in the center of the Karatsu Plain facing Karatsu Bay. It was discovered in 1924 when the owner of the land dug up the passage to the stone chamber while clearing a forest, and discovered the grave goods when he opened the sarcophagus inside. The horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south and is divided into a passageway and a rear chamber. The rear chamber is about 4 meters long, 2.25 meters wide and 2.34 meters high and gradually narrows towards the top, reaching a ceiling on consisting of three huge stones. It was constructed using broken basalt stones and granite, and the entire wall is painted with red pigment. Part of the western wall of the passageway has been destroyed, but what is thought to be the gate remains in its original state and is blocked by a single slab of stone. Inside the burial chamber are three stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]] made of flat slabs, with the first stone sarcophagus adjacent to the back wall having a cover stone, and the stone on the south side forming the northern wall of the second stone sarcophagus which is parallel to it, and the third stone sarcophagus placed separately, parallel to the western wall of the antechamber. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a round and a square [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'' in the first sarcophagus, cylindrical copperware, iron arrowheads, and a short armor in the second sarcophagus, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery in the third sarcophagus, as well as the bones of eight people. The Kofun is of a common form, but the style of the burial chamber and layout of the Sarcophaguss is unique in ''kofun'' which have been excavated to date. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The grave goods designated are a Saga Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031524/|title=佐賀文化財 database|language=ja |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Hamasaki Station|Hamasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Yokotashimo Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun, with an estimated diameter of about 30 meters, located on a hill about 60 meters above sea level at the eastern foot of Mount Kagamiyama, which is located almost in the center of the Karatsu Plain facing Karatsu Bay. It was discovered in 1924 when the owner of the land dug up the passage to the stone chamber while clearing a forest, and discovered the grave goods when he opened the sarcophagus inside. The horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south and is divided into a passageway and a rear chamber. The rear chamber is about 4 meters long, 2.25 meters wide and 2.34 meters high and gradually narrows towards the top, reaching a ceiling on consisting of three huge stones. It was constructed using broken basalt stones and granite, and the entire wall is painted with red pigment. Part of the western wall of the passageway has been destroyed, but what is thought to be the gate remains in its original state and is blocked by a single slab of stone. Inside the burial chamber are three stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]] made of flat slabs, with the first stone sarcophagus adjacent to the back wall having a cover stone, and the stone on the south side forming the northern wall of the second stone sarcophagus which is parallel to it, and the third stone sarcophagus placed separately, parallel to the western wall of the antechamber. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a round and a square [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'' in the first sarcophagus, cylindrical copperware, iron arrowheads, and a short armor in the second sarcophagus, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery in the third sarcophagus, as well as the bones of eight people. The Kofun is of a common form, but the style of the burial chamber and layout of the Sarcophaguss is unique in ''kofun'' which have been excavated to date. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The grave goods designated are a Saga Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031524/|title=佐賀文化財 database|language=ja |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Hamasaki Station|Hamasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031667/ Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yokotashimo Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yokotashimo Kofun]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yokotashimo Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Yokotashimo Kofun | native_name =横田下古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Saga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yokotashimo Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{Coord|33|26|04|N|130|02|01|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu, Saga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|26|04|N|130|02|01|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Yokotashimo Kofun'''|横田下古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the [[:en:Hamatama, Saga|Hamatama]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Karatsu, Saga|Karatsu]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/192012|title=横田下古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yokotashimo Kofun is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular Kofun, with an estimated diameter of about 30 meters, located on a hill about 60 meters above sea level at the eastern foot of Mount Kagamiyama, which is located almost in the center of the Karatsu Plain facing Karatsu Bay. It was discovered in 1924 when the owner of the land dug up the passage to the stone chamber while clearing a forest, and discovered the grave goods when he opened the sarcophagus inside. The horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south and is divided into a passageway and a rear chamber. The rear chamber is about 4 meters long, 2.25 meters wide and 2.34 meters high and gradually narrows towards the top, reaching a ceiling on consisting of three huge stones. It was constructed using broken basalt stones and granite, and the entire wall is painted with red pigment. Part of the western wall of the passageway has been destroyed, but what is thought to be the gate remains in its original state and is blocked by a single slab of stone. Inside the burial chamber are three stone [[:en:Sarcophagus|sarcophagi]] made of flat slabs, with the first stone sarcophagus adjacent to the back wall having a cover stone, and the stone on the south side forming the northern wall of the second stone sarcophagus which is parallel to it, and the third stone sarcophagus placed separately, parallel to the western wall of the antechamber. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a round and a square [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], ''[[magatama]]'' in the first sarcophagus, cylindrical copperware, iron arrowheads, and a short armor in the second sarcophagus, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pottery in the third sarcophagus, as well as the bones of eight people. The Kofun is of a common form, but the style of the burial chamber and layout of the Sarcophaguss is unique in ''kofun'' which have been excavated to date. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The grave goods designated are a Saga Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031524/|title=佐賀文化財 database|language=ja |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Hamasaki Station|Hamasaki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031667/ Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

It was discovered in 1924 when the owner of the land dug up the passage to the stone chamber while clearing a forest, and discovered the grave goods when he opened the sarcophagus inside. The horizontal-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south and is divided into a passageway and a rear chamber. The rear chamber is about 4 meters long, 2.25 meters wide and 2.34 meters high and gradually narrows towards the top, reaching a ceiling on consisting of three huge stones. It was constructed using broken basalt stones and granite, and the entire wall is painted with red pigment. Part of the western wall of the passageway has been destroyed, but what is thought to be the gate remains in its original state and is blocked by a single slab of stone. Inside the burial chamber are three stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi}} made of flat slabs, with the first stone sarcophagus adjacent to the back wall having a cover stone, and the stone on the south side forming the northern wall of the second stone sarcophagus which is parallel to it, and the third stone sarcophagus placed separately, parallel to the western wall of the antechamber. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include a round and a square {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, ''[[magatama]]'' in the first sarcophagus, cylindrical copperware, iron arrowheads, and a short armor in the second sarcophagus, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} pottery in the third sarcophagus, as well as the bones of eight people. The Kofun is of a common form, but the style of the burial chamber and layout of the Sarcophaguss is unique in ''kofun'' which have been excavated to date. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The grave goods designated are a Saga Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031524/|title=佐賀文化財 database|language=ja |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Hamasaki Station|en|Hamasaki Station|WD=Q5644129|lt=Hamasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.saga-otakara.jp/search/detail.html?cultureId=5349 Historical Cultural Archives of Saga] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031667/ Saga Prefecture home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Inside the burial chamber are three stone {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=sarcophagi|1=Sarcophagus}} made of flat slabs, with the first stone sarcophagus adjacent to the back wall having a cover stone, and the stone on the south side forming the northern wall of the second stone sarcophagus which is parallel to it, and the third stone sarcophagus placed separately, parallel to the western wall of the antechamber. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include a round and a square {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, ''[[magatama]]'' in the first sarcophagus, cylindrical copperware, iron arrowheads, and a short armor in the second sarcophagus, and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery in the third sarcophagus, as well as the bones of eight people. The Kofun is of a common form, but the style of the burial chamber and layout of the Sarcophaguss is unique in ''kofun'' which have been excavated to date. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> The grave goods designated are a Saga Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property. <ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://www.pref.saga.lg.jp/kiji0031524/|title=佐賀文化財 database|language=ja |publisher=Saga Prefecture |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
The tumulus is about a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Hamasaki Station|en|Hamasaki Station|WD=Q5644129|lt=Hamasaki Station|1=Hamasaki Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line|1=Chikuhi Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
